title: thrinax parviflora subsp. parviflora
intro: Thrinax parviflora subsp. parviflora, also known as the pygmy thatch palm or dwarf thatch palm, is a species of palm tree in the family Arecaceae. The plant is native to the Caribbean and parts of Central and South America, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Thrinax parviflora subsp. parviflora include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its use in treating various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant have been used to treat fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and it is also believed to have wound-healing properties, among other health benefits.
